title: epipactis × schmalhausenii nothosubsp. capellonensis
intro: Epipactis × schmalhausenii nothosubsp. capellonensis is a nothosubspecies of orchid, also known as a hybrid orchid, resulting from the cross between Epipactis helleborine and Epipactis schmalhausenii. This plant is used in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Epipactis × schmalhausenii nothosubsp. capellonensis include its potential to alleviate respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its ability to reduce inflammation and improve overall well-being. Herbal preparations of this plant can be consumed as teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used to support immune function and combat infections.
